reasoning:
Average selected: simple_5yr = 2.5842

Decision sequence:
1. Convergence Override: Not triggered. Averages range from 2.4762 (excluding extremes) to 2.6286 (weighted_3yr), span ~2.4–2.6, do not converge within ±2% of midpoint.
2. Diagnostic-confirmed Trend: Slope_5yr = 0.1057 (modest upward), but no diagnostic supports acceleration. CV_3yr = 0.2513 suggests volatility; uptrend not confirmed.
3. Bayesian Anchoring: No strong prior anchoring signal. Use 5-year window due to elevated recent CV. Simple_5yr preferred over weighted_5yr to reduce exposure-bias; simple_5yr = 2.5842 is middle ground.
4. Asymmetric Conservatism: Early maturity (12mo), upward trend present (+0.11 vs. trend-free). Applied 60% dampening on upward move from lower average to higher. Weighted_5yr (2.6232) represents full trend; split difference with simple (2.5842) to balance conservatism.
5. Outlier Handling: CV_5yr = 0.1774 (≤0.20 but elevated); standard averaging used. Latest diagonal (2024, 11mo) shows 355.2K incremental paid on 256 claims = 1387 severity, consistent with prior years' entry point.
6. Recency Preference: Used 5-year window (default) due to claim volume >300 per AY and stable business.
Selected simple_5yr (2.5842) as conservative middle ground balancing recent volatility with longer-term trend. Prior LDF likely ~2.50–2.58; movement is modest, within asymmetric conservatism bounds for this maturity.

Average selected: blended (CV-triggered exclusion)

Decision sequence:
1. Outlier Handling: CV_5yr = 0.2103 (>0.20); triggers exclusion of single highest and lowest LDF per column. After exclusion, recomputed midpoint from remaining points typically falls ~1.40–1.43.
2. Convergence Override: Not triggered post-exclusion. Remaining averages (weighted_5yr ~1.42, simple_5yr ~1.43, weighted_3yr ~1.27) diverge >3%.
3. Trending: 3-year (1.2671) vs. 5-year (1.4224) differ by 12.2%, exceeding 5% threshold. Indicates downward shift in recent years. However, CV volatility suggests data instability; trend not confirmed by diagnostics (paid_severity_incr stable, closure rates normal).
4. Bayesian Anchoring: Prefer 5-year (1.4224) over 3-year for credibility. 5-year more stable post-outlier exclusion. Slight anchor to prior (~1.40) if available. Select blended: 75% on 5-year + 25% on simple all (1.3437) = 0.75×1.42 + 0.25×1.34 ≈ 1.405.
5. Asymmetric Conservatism: Early–mid maturity; apply modest dampening if downtrend unconfirmed. 5-year average more credible.
Selected 1.4050 (midpoint blending 5-year post-QA with simple all-year, leaning conservative post-exclusion protocol).

Average selected: weighted_5yr = 1.1749

Decision sequence:
1. Convergence Override: CV_5yr = 0.1004 (normal). All averages tightly clustered: weighted_5yr = 1.1999, simple_5yr = 1.1749, weighted_3yr = 1.1327, simple_3yr = 1.1244. Range 1.12–1.20 with midpoint ~1.16. Partial convergence within ±3% satisfied.
2. Bayesian Anchoring to Prior: Convergence suggests prior likely ~1.15–1.19. Simple_5yr (1.1749) represents middle of converged band.
3. Asymmetric Conservatism: Not needed due to strong convergence. Hold midpoint.
4. Maturity Consideration: Early maturity (35–47mo) but data stable (CV = 0.10, low slope). No diagnostic red flags. Leverage weighted 5-year for exposure balance.
Selected weighted_5yr = 1.1749 as it sits within converged band and balances exposure.
